stories.title: HMRC Consultation on Lower-Value Tax Debts
stories.summary: As part of the Tax Update package announced on 23 June 2026, the government launched a consultation on proposals to tackle lower-value tax debts from customers. Details are covered in Issue 145 of Agent Update.

stories.title: Home Office Launches Consultation on Future of Undercover Policing Inquiry
stories.summary: The Home Office has launched a time-limited public consultation on the future structure, scope and format of the Undercover Policing Inquiry, open for responses from 16 July 2026 until 20 August 2026.

stories.title: Organised Immigration Crime Arrests Up 74%
stories.summary: The Home Office reports at least 1,900 arrests relating to organised immigration crime between April 2025 and March 2026, a 74% increase compared to the previous year, as published in the Border Security Commander's annual report data.

stories.title: Political Peerages Announced — July 2026
stories.summary: The Home Office has published a list of new Political Peerages of the United Kingdom for Life, with the announcement made on 17 July 2026.

stories.title: FCDO Annual Report and Accounts 2025–2026 Published
stories.summary: The Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office has published its annual report and accounts for the financial year 2025 to 2026, providing a comprehensive overview of its activities and expenditure.

stories.title: UK Aid Spending Set to Fall by Around 42%
stories.summary: UK aid spending is expected to fall by around 42% — or £6.5 billion — from a record high in 2023 to a historic low in 2027–28, as the UK reduces its aid budget from 0.5% to 0.3% of gross national income, according to a new watchdog report.

stories.title: UK's Modern Development Approach Published
stories.summary: The FCDO has published its updated development approach for July 2026, committing that at least 90% of FCDO's work will mainstream gender equality, and setting out strengthened development priorities.

stories.title: DWP Allows Winter Fuel Payments Opt-Out in England, Wales and Northern Ireland
stories.summary: Customers in England, Wales and Northern Ireland can now contact the DWP to opt out of receiving future Winter Fuel Payments, as detailed in the HMRC Agent Update Issue 145.

stories.title: Carers Allowance Transitional Protection for Scottish Customers
stories.summary: A new agency agreement has been published between the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions and Scottish Ministers in respect of Carers Allowance transitional protection for Scottish customers.

agencies.name: DEFRA
agencies.description: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs

stories.title: Government Publishes Future of Rural England Report
stories.summary: DEFRA has published the Future of Rural England Report, setting out the challenges facing rural communities and outlining government commitments to ensure policy delivers for rural areas across England.

stories.title: Government Response to OEP Environmental Improvement Report
stories.summary: The government has published its response to the Office for Environmental Protection's 2026 report on progress in improving England's natural environment from 2024 to 2025.

stories.title: RPC Scrutiny of DEFRA Biodiversity Net Gain Regulations
stories.summary: The Regulatory Policy Committee has published a statement updating on its scrutiny of DEFRA's impact assessment for biodiversity net gain regulations, assessing the regulatory burden on businesses.

stories.title: MoD Launches Cancer Study for Armed Forces Community
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence has launched a new study into cancer incidence among 1.4 million serving personnel and veterans, stepping up efforts to improve the long-term health of the Armed Forces community.

stories.title: Major General Phil Carter to Become Interim Chief Defence Medical
stories.summary: Major General Phil Carter, currently Surgeon General of the UK Armed Forces, will take on the role of interim Chief Defence Medical following the retirement of Air Marshal Sir Paul Nichols.

stories.title: Government Brings British Steel into Public Ownership
stories.summary: The government has concluded it is in the public interest to take ownership of British Steel, bringing the company into public ownership to protect the future of UK steelmaking and preserve jobs.

stories.title: Dame Jayne-Anne Gadhia Appointed Chair of Financial Reporting Council
stories.summary: The Department for Business and Trade has announced that Dame Jayne-Anne Gadhia has been appointed as the new Chair of the Financial Reporting Council, effective from 16 July 2026.

stories.title: North of England Launches First Joint Trade Mission to India
stories.summary: Supported by DBT, which reports UK-India trade reached £47.9bn in 2025 (up 10%), northern mayors joined businesses and universities on the first-ever joint trade mission from the North of England to India.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: DSIT Public Engagement Survey 2025/2026 Published
stories.summary: DSIT has published official statistics from its Public Engagement Survey 2025/2026, measuring how UK adults are engaging with science and technology in their day-to-day lives.

stories.title: Teenagers from Age 15 Should Receive Free MenB Vaccine, Experts Recommend
stories.summary: UK health advisers are now recommending that all teenagers should be offered a free meningitis B (MenB) vaccine at around age 15 — a significant change from previous guidance — in a bid to better protect young people.

stories.title: All Newborns in England to Be Screened for Spinal Muscular Atrophy
stories.summary: England is expanding newborn screening to include spinal muscular atrophy (SMA), with nearly 72% of newborns due to be tested from October under a pilot programme announced in April and now receiving broader rollout.
